body.title: Autocompact send interrupt signals before command
body.result: Added three interrupt Enter signals before compact command in skills/autocompact/SKILL.md — deployed to repo root, reverted incorrect ~/.claude/skills edit
body.intention: I intend that /autocompact uses interrupt signals (three Enters) before sending the compact command to guarantee the command survives concurrent user typing.
body.description:
The /autocompact skill currently sends the compact command via tmux without interrupt signals, so if you're typing when the command is sent, it can get inserted mid-word and lost. Proposed fix: send three Enter signals first (visible interruption that breaks any ongoing input), then send the compact command, then Enter. Trades UX smoothness for robustness — the interruption is visible but guarantees the command always fires.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/autocompact/SKILL.md

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** Only the tmux send-keys pattern in Step 2 of /autocompact (the two send-keys calls at lines ~51-53). The preserve string and compact logic unchanged.

**Completion criteria:** /autocompact sends three Enter signals before the compact command string via tmux; verified by reading SKILL.md and testing one autocompaction to confirm interruption signals fire.
